findings.title: No agent integration exists in the codebase — this is a pure REST API, not agent-native
findings.suggested_fix: No fix required for this review scope. If agent-native architecture is a future goal, expose an OpenAPI schema that describes all parse/lineage endpoints so tool-call generators can discover them automatically.

findings.title: Parser fix does not affect any agent-accessible surface — no tool definitions, MCP bindings, or LLM API calls exist
The /warnings endpoint now surfaces fewer false-positive warnings after the case-sensitivity fix, but this improvement is not queryable by structured filters (no severity=, file= query params)
findings.suggested_fix: Add optional ?severity= and ?source_id= query params to GET /warnings so that a future agent can filter parse warnings programmatically without receiving the full list and filtering client-side.

findings.title: No OpenAPI schema annotations — all 14 REST endpoints lack response_model declarations, making automated tool-call generation or schema export unreliable
findings.suggested_fix: Add Pydantic response_model= to each router decorator so FastAPI generates a complete, accurate OpenAPI spec. This is the minimum baseline for agent-native tool discovery.

residual_risks: The codebase has no agent integration today. The parser fix is correct and complete for its stated purpose (case-insensitive temp view lookup). No agent-native regression risk from this change.

If an agent were to call GET /lineage or GET /lineage/paths for columns in a Databricks SQL notebook that used mixed-case temp view names, it would previously receive an incomplete or wrong graph. This fix silently corrects that — there is no API version bump or changelog entry that would signal to a consuming agent that results have changed.

The @modelcontextprotocol/sdk package appears in frontend/node_modules but is not wired to any server-side handler. If MCP integration is planned, the parser fix enables correct lineage for a broader set of real-world SQL files, which increases the value of exposing parse_sql as an MCP tool.
testing_gaps: No integration test covers the round-trip: upload a ZIP containing a Databricks SQL notebook with a mixed-case CREATE TEMPORARY VIEW, call POST /sources/{id}/refresh, then GET /lineage?table=...&column=... and assert the graph traces to the real source — not the temp view alias.

test_temp_view_with_mixed_case_in_databricks_notebook passes a raw SQL string directly to parse_sql(); it bypasses the HTTP layer entirely. An API-level test via TestClient is absent for this specific regression.

GET /search is case-sensitive on node IDs (uses `q_lower in node.lower()`); there is no test verifying that search results are consistent before and after temp view resolution for mixed-case view names.
